claim: Verified explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence schema, readiness contract, and tests implement scope separation between project, external, user, and unknown evidence, with validation requiring project-scoped evidence for existing-project facts.

evidence.summary: Confirmed DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA defines scope field as exact union: 'project' | 'external' | 'user' | 'unknown', with description explaining each type.
evidence.why: Primary schema definition proves scope separation is built into the type system.

evidence.summary: Confirmed VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES is new Set(['project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown']) - runtime enforcement of valid scope values.
evidence.why: Shows runtime validation matches compile-time schema.

evidence.summary: Confirmed evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator checks for path/line fields when scope === 'project', preventing external URLs from masquerading as project evidence.
evidence.why: Core validation prevents external sources from satisfying project scope.

evidence.summary: Confirmed projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function: if no project-scoped evidence exists for existing-project claim, returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.
evidence.why: Critical enforcement logic that prevents external evidence from satisfying project facts.

evidence.summary: Confirmed test case 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' verifies blocker is correctly generated.
evidence.why: Test proves the validation logic works as intended.

evidence.summary: Confirmed test 'cross-check PRD readiness requires every evidence agent result' checks all three agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) must complete.
Confirmed PRD readiness check iterates requiredEvidenceAgents and generates EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blocker if any agent missing.
evidence.why: Shows evidence policy is enforced at PRD readiness gate.

risks: If validation logic is bypassed or schema constraints are weakened in future changes, external evidence could incorrectly satisfy project-fact requirements.
If tests are disabled or schema validation is removed, scope separation enforcement would fail silently.

result.notes: Independent verification confirms explorer claim. Schema, validation functions, and tests all consistently implement scope separation. Project facts require project-scoped evidence with path/line locators. External evidence is explicitly blocked from satisfying project facts through multiple layers: type schema, runtime validation, and test coverage.
